item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the image comes from here with no indication it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I second you here. While the personality of the "soul" may add a bit of silliness, it is certainly something that the foundation would treat with complete sincerity.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: I must have read this five times over, I don't know, three years. Still one of the creepiest SCPs I've ever seen, and so… softly disturbing.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Got a chance to talk to Silber about this skip, and his headcanon for it. Apparently, it was his opinion that the singer containted within 1523 may or may not have been a member of The Fifth Dimension at somepoint. As such, I am recommending the following edits in addition to a link to the Fifthist hub:

Changing this:

SCP-1523 is an orange-brown incense coil. While SCP-1523 is burning, the object displays sapience; it is responsive to verbal stimuli and capable of speech, using a voice identical to that of a musician named █████████████1 The object possesses complete memory of █████'s life2. The Foundation cannot determine whether SCP-1523 held the same personality traits in life; its current demeanor, including its obsession with religion, may result from its current form.

To this:

SCP-1523 is an orange-brown incense coil. While SCP-1523 is burning, the object displays sapience; it is responsive to verbal stimuli and capable of speech, using a voice identical to that of a musician named █████████████3 The object possesses complete memory of █████'s life4. The Foundation cannot determine whether SCP-1523 held the same personality traits in life; its current demeanor, including its obsession with religion, may result from its current form.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. Name withheld due to Operation Stargazer protocol.
2. Born in 1951, █████████████ achieved fame in the 1970s as a rhythm-and-blues singer, known for his characteristic low voice and range.
3. Name withheld due to Operation Stargazer protocol.
4. Born in 1951, █████████████ achieved fame in the 1970s as a rhythm-and-blues singer, known for his characteristic low voice and range, as well as his brief time spent as a member of the band The Fifth Dimension.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This got disturbing in the second interview quick. The last sentence or two was absolute horror, and the implications of it are freaky to say the least. I enjoy the subtle body horror of it being inside of you forever after you smell-speak to it.

10/10 would be creeped out again

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is exactly what I was thinking and you articulated it perfectly. I'm upvoting for the same reasons.

item.title: Re: Al Green was born in 1946.
item.content:encoded: Actually It's probably : http://tinyurl.com/hz9p7of (SPOILER)

item.title: Re: Al Green was born in 1946.
item.content:encoded:
This is a massive necropost, but it also can't be Luther Vandross. Both he and Green were known for their falsettos. The year of birth is Vandross, but—

"achieved fame in the 1970s as a rhythm-and-blues singer, known for his characteristic low voice and range."

—means this is Barry White. Born in 1944.

The singer isn't based on anyone. It's a generic soul/R&B artist

Soul music is a passion of mine, and thus this is my favorite skip. Sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one is so great and it honestly deserves more attention. And I say that fully aware it's already sitting comfortably at 100+. I just think it's that good.

No, brother, without a soul. Ever seen an anthill? That ant doesn't have room for a soul in there. If a man has no soul, he can keep building his anthill just fine. Right now the man I was is occupied with [REDACTED] and you couldn't tell the difference if you met it, unless you were giving it a throat exam. [laughs] He's quiet in there, but he wriggles around like he can't get comfortable.

This is a line that stayed with me ever since I read this back in… like, 2013, probably? It raises lots of interesting questions while at the same time expanding upon and reinforcing the Fifth Church canon (the idea of people expelling or ejecting their souls as a method of self-improvement is, to me at least, very creepy and something I think later Fifthist entries dropped unnecessarily).

I place this in the holy trinity of Fifthist skips, along with 1982 and 1425. Part of what's great, of course, is that we go from creepy religious occult horror to the very, very real horror of a sexual predator. The supernatural elements somehow heighten this guy's character as an utter creep, and frankly makes this entry stand out from most other Fifthist offerings. I think horror needs to connect to something familiar to make it truly scary. Harassment like this is, sadly, all too familiar for too many people, which lends the Fifthist stuff greater weight and impact.

I don't know what else to say, I just really friggin' love this skip. It's perfectly awful and I've yet to see anything quite like it on the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, and here I found the culprit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: u ruined it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And I just changed it back, because I missed the old one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Silberescher changed it on 03-17-2015, from "Soul Brother" to the new one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm pretty sure this used to have a mainlist name other than 'Sexually Harassing R&B Smoke Monster'. When'd it change and what was it before?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, we use those two words for the definitions Jekeled mentioned. You can check it in the the guide. Sentient is defined as "able to perceive or feel things", while sapient as defined as "being or appearing wise". Their not exactly the same as the dictionary, but they imply the same meanings we use in tagging articles. (Dog is sentient, but not sapient since it can't consider something like government)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know this is a terrible necro, but for the record, sentience and sapience can pretty much be used interchangeably. Look it up.
There you have it. Good job. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was just about to come post this.
Goddammit, Scan.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
RIGHT ABOUT NOW

THE FUNK SCP-1523 CHECK IT OUT NOW

item.title: Re: Al Green was born in 1946.
item.content:encoded:
According to my research, it's probably

Warning! Original Research
Close

Luther Vandross

item.title: Re: Al Green was born in 1946.
item.content:encoded: Who said anything about Al Green? We're talking about █████████████ here.

item.title: Al Green was born in 1946.
item.content:encoded: The footnote says 1951. Seems like a conflicting detail.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That second interview. Totally creeplol.

item.title: Re: Member Post
item.content:encoded: Thou makest a good point.

item.title: Member Post
item.content:encoded: For the record, now that the Fifthists have their own spot on the GoI page, you should probably get rid of the second footnote. It was annoying before (thou must always show, brother, and not tell) and now it's unnecessary. People should be able to figure out what the Fifthists are by looking at the objects, and barring that, by looking at the GoI page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fifth church is slowly becoming my new favourite GOI.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Odd that you aren't more paranoid about the water.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Al Green-cum-incense is one creepy cat.

You know how when you go to the home of a heavy smoker, and even if you don't stay for long, your hair and clothes still smell like an ashtray for the rest of the day? Yeah, well what if that smoke was a lecherous dead guy? Just snuggling his perverted ghost self right into your pores and worming down into your underwear. First item in a long time that actually made my skin crawl. I want a shower.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
conserve him so that he doesn't shuffle off his mortal coil.

groooooan

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is absolutely nothing creepy about the SCP as a whole.
The way he/it talks, though, is exceptionally creepy, in an "I need to go take a shower thanks to listening to that" sort of way.
Well done; and while another mention of the Fifthist thing might be nice, I do sort of like the concept that they're just this quasi-mystical cult that a bunch of celebrities got into for no reason. (Thought at first this was going to be one big dig at Scientology, which would also have earned an upvote from me, but it wasn't and I'm quite cool with that)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm glad to hear it. I wasn't planning for the Fifth Church to be a recurring entity, but maybe they'll find a niche that makes them worth revisiting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's well below the general threshold for silliness in an SCP, since its effects should be taken seriously in-universe.

And the Fifth Church has nothing to do with Christianity, except that its trappings are influenced by various other religions and sects.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nicely done. I knew you couldn't trust rhythm-and-blues coils of incense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know how I didn't catch that, thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hear, hear!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At least that makes me feel like I've succeeded. Illuminatus is one of my favorite novels.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just noticed something-the picture caption says its not active, but I can see it burning.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. I kinda dig the church as a possible group of interest, as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the emotional feel of this, but I'm not sure how I like it as an SCP.

Nice surreal vibe, real nice. It's like I'm reading from Illuminatus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The fact that I thought of "socially inappropriate Al Green smoke" before I read the above comment is exactly why I am downvoting.

EDIT: To clarify. It seemed a bit silly to me. Also the 'occult organization inside a christian church' idea is a bit overdone.

/shrug

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wasn't too impressed until I read the logs. That's when it truly came alive. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Strange, yet oddly sinister. I approve, I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really, really clever and I love the background on this thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved in sandbox, loved here too. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So then state that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's only lit for enough time to speak with it or test its properties, which is no more than an hour per use, and incense coils are long and slow-burning.

His lifespan is limited, however, so they will have to conserve him so that he doesn't shuffle off his mortal coil.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It only speaks when it's burned but you don't mention it regenerating, wouldn't we be hesitant to burn an SCP object that cannot heal/regenerate lost mass?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am absolutely befuddled as to what to make of "Sexual Harassing Al Green Incense". Definitely not downvoting, however.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvote removed for now.

Edit: I actually am upvoting this now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Your new suggestions have been noted and acted upon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry about that, took a few seconds to get the requisite material.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Your suggestion has been taken into consideration.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

> lit witb

I think you mean with.

> The object never burn unattended, under penalty of severe disciplinary action.

English learn you. Site English. Good English must before I kill you.

> The incense, whose coiled shape provides a long burn time

Unprofessional tone

> it displays sentience

I believe you're referring to sapience. Sentience is awareness. Sapience is intelligence.

As all these errors were fixed…quite some time ago, I upvoted (also quote some time ago).

(Sorry Silber.)
